newsSinceJun6: No material news. Only items in the window: a small director sale (Sven Wehrwein, 2,000 shares, ~$154K, transaction dated June 4 and reported around June 5-6) and an upcoming Craig-Hallum-hosted investor meeting in Toronto/Montreal. No earnings, contracts, design wins, M&A, capacity news, or analyst target changes. Backdrop unchanged: Q1 2026 record revenue $139.3M (+10.4% y/y), adj EPS $0.54, cautious FY26 guide of 6-8% revenue growth; mean analyst price targets sit at or below the current $74.98 price.

sources.label: GuruFocus - Insider sell: Sven Wehrwein sells 2,000 shares of PRLB
Benzinga - Proto Labs board member sold $154K in company stock
Yahoo Finance - Record CNC quarter and cautious 2026 guidance
MarketBeat - PRLB forecast and price targets

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already pushed PRLB down for the +46% YTD rerating without new AI-chain evidence; nothing genuinely new has emerged since June 6 (only a small director sale and a roadshow), so hold at rank 81 with mild negative drift on this strict latent-node screen.
verdicts.bull: On-demand digital manufacturing (CNC, 3D printing, injection molding) gives AI hardware builders a fast prototyping path for custom thermal and mechanical parts before incumbent supply chains scale; Q1 was a record CNC quarter with adj EPS at a five-year high.
verdicts.neutral: Solid execution but no disclosed AI-specific revenue or design wins; FY26 guidance of 6-8% growth is cautious, and at $74.98 the stock trades above the mean analyst target, leaving the latent-AI optionality fully theoretical and increasingly priced.
verdicts.bear: A +46% YTD run on a low-single-digit-growth prototyping business compresses residual alpha; insider selling at these levels and a thin two-analyst coverage base mean any macro-industrial slowdown or AI-prototyping disappointment hits an already-rich multiple.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) showing AI/data-center-related order mix or a named design-win disclosure; near term, incremental visibility from the Craig-Hallum investor meetings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding into a thesis with no confirmed AI revenue: if Q2 lands within the cautious guide with no AI evidence, the YTD gain unwinds toward sub-$60 analyst targets.
